tasks.title: Shaw, Jeffrey - PPWK BENE UPDATES
tasks.notes:
06/03/2026 - I came across some information regarding their daughter, Kristen, passing away recently.

Kristen Millet, age 44, of Grove City, Ohio passed away in her home on Friday, May 29, 2026 after a courageous two and a half year struggle with Glioblastoma. Kristen was born on November 29, 1981, in Toledo, Ohio. She graduated with honors from St. Ursula Academy in Ottawa Hills, Ohio in 1999. She graduated from The Ohio State University earning a B.S. in education and an M.Ed., in special education. Kristen also participated in OSU’s Accelerated Licensure Program for her Principalship certification. OSU always held a special place in Kristen’s heart, where she spent many happy years. While there, Kristen was an active member of Alpha Xi Delta Sorority. Best of all, she met fellow Buckeye and future husband, Wes.

Kristen is survived by her beloved husband, Wesley Millet; her cherished children Riley and Mason; her devoted parents, Carol Fornof and Jeff Shaw; loving in-laws, Fred and Marcia Millet; and aunts, uncles, and many cousins. She was preceded in death by her maternal grandparents, Kenneth and Kathryn Brancheau.

Kristen was known for her vibrant spirit, sharp intelligence, and quick wit. She possessed a rare ability to invoke laughter in others while also making them think critically. Her humor and insight left a lasting impression on everyone she met. Kristen had an exceptionally curated sense of style and fashion that reflected her individuality, confidence, and artistic eye. Whether through her words, her work, or her creativity, she brought beauty, thoughtfulness, and authenticity into the world around her. She especially loved expressing herself through her Etsy shop, Witherwill Studios.

Kristen began her teaching career at Jackson Middle School in Grove City. After gaining valuable experience, both in the classroom and as a district-wide leader, Kristen proudly assumed the role of assistant principal at Hawthorne Elementary in the Westerville City School District and subsequently served as a consultant for the Ohio Department of Education. Kristen devoted her life to helping students learn, grow, and believe in themselves. As a respected leader and expert in special education pedagogy, she was a fierce advocate for children with disabilities and their families. She approached her work with patience, wisdom, compassion, and a strong conviction that every student deserved to be seen, valued, and given every opportunity to succeed.

Her greatest classroom, however, was her home. Kristen took immense pride in her children and found deep joy in their academic, artistic and athletic accomplishments. She taught Riley (15) and Mason (11) through her example of perseverance, humor, and unconditional love. One of Kristen’s greatest joys was spending priceless family time on Hilton Head Island.

As Jesus taught with compassion, grace, and love, Kristen lived her life in much the same way - lifting others up, guiding those in need, and leaving a lasting impact on the hearts of so many.

Kristen spent several weeks at Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital where she encountered an engaging, encouraging and loving staff who helped her accomplish extraordinary feats in spite of her physical limitations. Prior to her passing, Kristen requested memorial contributions be made to Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital. https://wexnermedical.osu.edu/locations/dodd-rehabilitation-hospital

A memorial service to honor her life will be held at a later date.

Kristen will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered, by her family, friends, students, and community.

Arrangements by Schoedinger Grove City. Visit [www.schoedinger.com](http://www.schoedinger.com/) to share a special memory or extend condolences. (kso)

06/05/26- Pulled FID IRA/HSA and FID TOD for both Carol and Jeff. Looks like Carol has a blackrock 529 I need to look into to. (ctb)

TO DO:
**-keep Carol & Jeff's information separate!  Do not mail anything together.**
-run list of all accounts
-prep bene paperwork
-hold off on sending until we get go ahead from BES

tasks.notes:
Forms Requiring SupervisionThe list below shows the forms that require supervisory review by a supervisor or designee. To ensure proper supervision, please review the list and select the appropriate supervision path (“Requires Supervision” or “Does not require Supervision”) when submitting additional paperwork in CLIC® for an existing account. Note that if any form in the batch is included on the list, the entire batch requires supervision. Any form that requires a principal or supervisor signature must also be submitted through a supervised trade key.

Forms requiring supervision generally fall into these categories:

Suitability Forms
All versions of Cambridge’s Client Information and Suitability forms, including Trust/Entity, Group, Additional Signers, etc.
Resident Alien Questionnaire, Politically Exposed Person Questionnaire, and US Resident Client Living Abroad Questionnaire
Applications and account opening documents
Fund company applications (annuity, alternative investments, mutual funds, etc.), custodian account applications, Third Party Manager applications, checkwriting and margin applications, change of broker-dealer forms, record keeper service agreements, etc.
Cambridge product disclosures and suitability rationale documents
Annuity Disclosure, Alternative Investment Disclosure, 529 Plan Disclosure, etc.
Best Interest Analysis forms, RightBRIDGE Annuity Wizard, and RightBRIDGE Product Profiler
Investment Exchange Disclosures
Management Agreements, including fee arrangements
Financial Planning and Consulting documents
Legal Documents and Authorized Signer forms
Documents that indicate who is authorized to act on investment accounts, such as Corporate Resolution forms, Trustee Certification of Investment Powers, Power of Attorney documents, etc.
Account maintenance and supplemental forms
Margin agreements, options agreements, check writing applications
For directly held accounts, sponsor maintenance forms requiring supervision include those with financial or control implications—such as beneficiary updates, withdrawal requests to third parties, addition of trading authority, and any forms requiring a Medallion Signature Guarantee
While Cambridge does not require supervision of routine maintenance forms (e.g., address changes or standard first party banking information) we do require that they be maintained in the client file for books and records purposes. Additionally, the sponsor company may have specific submission and approval policies that need to be followed. It is important to note that address changes direct at sponsors do not typically carry over to Cambridge systems.

tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE TEMPLATES
tasks.notes:
**✅** ** Adding Templates toRightBRIDGE**

**BeforeYou Begin:**

There are **16 models** to add

Template IDs: **1489–1504**

* * *

**Steps:**

1. **Log     into CIR2.**
2. On
     the left-hand side, **click “RightBRIDGE.”**
3. Under
     **Investment Wizard Tools**, select **“Template Management.”**
4. Click
     **“Add Shared Template.”**
5. Enter
     the first **Template ID** (starting with 1489).
6. Click
     **“Save Template.”**
    - ⚠️ Important: You
      must hit **Save** before moving to the next template.
7. Repeat
     steps **4–6** for each ID through **1504.**
8. After
     all templates are added, confirm that **all 16 templates** appear in
     the list.

* * *

**OngoingMaintenance:**

- This
     is a **manual process.**
- When
     model changes occur, **Beamer will update** the RightBRIDGE templates.
- You
     will receive a **new shared template number** that must be added
     following the same process above.
- **For     future updates:**
     Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.
- It
     goes quickly, and this ensures **nothing is missed** or left outdated.

* * *

💬 **Need help?**

I’m happy to walk through these steps if needed — and for remote team members,
we can screen share to go through it together!

**Forfuture updates:**
Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.  It
goes quickly, and this ensures nothing is missed or left outdated.

tasks.notes:
**LASTUPDATED: 1/15/24 (zwb)** 

**It isimportant to note that the Rightbridge (RB) platform is onlyavailable to licensed admins/advisors. If you are not licensed you will beunable to complete this process** 

**Rightbridge isalso semi-frequently updated slightly so things may look different then theimages posted below** 

**Zoom in ifunable to read the images due to bluriness** 

1. First, login to https://www.cir2.com/ and
     navigate to the RB tool in the tool bar
2. Once you have entered the RB
     platform you will be presented with 4 options. We are currently not
     concerned with the Annuity or Investment Wizard's so dis regard these two
     options.

1. From this page you can select
     previous cases so you can edit them or copy them, but we are interested in
     the "Create New Case" tab under the product profiler.
2. This will launch the client
     information tab requiring information found on either RT, CLIC, or in the
     RB template created for you. If the client is married the spouses info
     will be required as well.
3. Next, the system will request a
     product to be validated which includes investment objectives, time
     horizon, product (advisory/commission account), amount, and fee. Check the
     RB template as well as CLIC for this information.
4. We then need to select the
     account(s) that we are transferring from, which will create additional
     pages for each account. The 3 I have selected are the most common we use
     when transferring to a traditional IRA. We also use "Cash/Current
     Income" for new ROTH IRAs frequently, but this does not require
     additional info besides the amount.
    1. 401k:
        1. If you
       know the name of the 401k plan, you can search for it to input known
       information, otherwise fill the account fees to the best of your
       ability. Typically, we don't have access to participant fees.
        2. As for
       the account features, below is a general template to follow as this
       applies to most people that want to transfer their money to us. However,
       adjust as needed.
    2. Advisory
      IRA:
        1. For
       advisory accounts, look at the statements for the account that is
       transferring for fee information. While the fee % most likely won't be
       listed, we can find the amount pulled and do the math to determine the
       fee %. (If you are unsure you can discuss with the advisor)
        2. For the
       fund fee, you can guesstimate the average if there are a large amount of
       mutual funds, or lookup the funds to calculate the amount. If it is all
       stocks then the fund fee is 0%.
        3. For
       account feature, adjust as needed.
    3. Brokerage
      IRA: 
        1. Brokerage
       IRAs have the same account features as Advisory, but the fee questions
       are different. Update as needed based off the statements or info from
       advisor.

1. The financial details page only
     has NIA currently which should be listed in the template or CLIC
2. Client preferences is a case by
     case basis, although you can find some of this info in CLIC and the
     following images are the typical responses for our clients. Discuss with
     the advisor if you have questions.
    1. Risk tolerance should be available
      in CLIC, liquidity is the focus unless we are doing an annuity, and for
      advice we put someone else managing it a majority of the time.
    2. Adjust fee sensitivity if you find
      it relevant, and insurance need should be "The client does not
      desire a product with death benefits" unless we are doing an annuity
      or insurance product. This RB is only focused on the one account, so it
      will give you an error if you want death benefits with an advisory
      account because you are unable to meet this need.
    3. For form CRS delivery date put the
      day the ppwk was signed or is going to be signed.
3. On the "Results" page,
     you may be required to provide additional notes based on which product(s)
     you are moving from, the new account you are opening, and how the
     questions were answered.
    1. Below is a typical error that
      shows up along with the usual response when a new client is switching
      advisors.

“Please provide additional rationale for
the selected recommendation” – Not happy with current advisor/investment
options

    1. Below
      is an example of a more in depth error that may occur, the test client is
      55 and thus the info is relevant to their situation. Discuss with advisor
      if you need clarification for the notes.

“Please
provide additional rationale for the selected recommendation” – Client has
additional outside accounts to pull from, and is not concerned with potential penalties
and would rather have broader investment options.

1. Once 
     you have completed all required notes, Click on the case summary button on
     the left of the screen.
2. Click the dropdown arrow for
     "Product Profiler" and select "Preview Product Profiler
     PDF" to download the RB. Provide to whoever needs the RB and copy to
     the template used to request to a RT note.

**A fewkey reminders:**

- For **advisory     accounts**, since we use **Nick Raiche and Horizons** in our model
     management selection, we should be selecting **Option 3 or 4** for
     objectives & preferences.

Prefers a broad mix of investment with oversight form an external
fund manager or team of managers that can actively make portfolio changes

Prefers a higher level of oversight beyond fund managers, including
fund screening and control over fund reallocation.

- For **commission-based,     directly held accounts** (mostly buy & hold), RightBRIDGE should
     reflect that. Please be sure to choose **one of the two circled options**
     for those.

The client would like to make their own decisions

The client will make their own decision, but would like
suggestions

-

tasks.title: Beard, Brian - PENNMUTUAL SURRENDER
tasks.notes:
08/13/2025 - FJD review cover noted Cash Out Rachel PM WL and Leah's PM WL - send forms to Brian Beard. The statement is attached to this activity. I'm not familiar with Penn Mutual and not clear if both Brian and Andrea have separate policies? The statement that was given to me after the review meeting was Penn Mutual Policy 0082705460

Rachel's parents to cash out Penn Mutual whole life policies and distribute funds to their daughters (Leah and Rachel (ejd)

08/14/2025 - There are two separate policies - one for Leah & one for Rachel. I called and spoke with Sarah to verifiy surrender forms for their specific policies and PennMutual is telling me that **Frank needs his licensing updated and is currently not active**.  I was able to get information regarding both Leah and Rachel's policies.  I will send over the surrender forms to Brian, as he is the owner of them - we turned off the monthly auto premium payment - should they change their minds and keep the policies, APL will kick in after 60 days to keep policy from lapsing.  ALSO - they say it is a 15-18 day processing turnaround time. She also said that they will calculate cost basis and they MAY receive tax doc for reportable transaction.  And since they removed from auto pay, they will receive a quarterly bill via the mail, which they can disregard.

Completed forms can be emailed to lifeservicesrequests@pennmutual.com.

I cannot find the form on the site - reached out and requested the form that Sarah said I needed:

**Fixed Products**: Use Form PM6529FIXED (rev 01/25). (kso)

08/18/2025 - Sent paper forms to Brian via USPS regular mail. (kso)

Maverick is having several employees let go or quit and go elsewhere.  Tim wants to get a new retirement plan set up so we discussed either IND-K or SEP IRA.  He will talk with Christian about it and we can get it set up once its decided what direction to go.  Tim has $360k gross consulting contract from iRock coming into Maverick Mindset and $50k of expenses.  He is getting another client for $40k fee.  Tim is on payroll now with Maverick Mindset and Christian is talking about switching it to s-corp to save on self employment tax.  Tim wants to buy this farm in Burton.  I sent him Steve Weihl's contact info so they can talk about farm financing options.

items.title: Shaw, Jeffrey - PPWK BENE UPDATES
items.notes:
06/03/2026 - I came across some information regarding their daughter, Kristen, passing away recently.

Kristen Millet, age 44, of Grove City, Ohio passed away in her home on Friday, May 29, 2026 after a courageous two and a half year struggle with Glioblastoma. Kristen was born on November 29, 1981, in Toledo, Ohio. She graduated with honors from St. Ursula Academy in Ottawa Hills, Ohio in 1999. She graduated from The Ohio State University earning a B.S. in education and an M.Ed., in special education. Kristen also participated in OSU’s Accelerated Licensure Program for her Principalship certification. OSU always held a special place in Kristen’s heart, where she spent many happy years. While there, Kristen was an active member of Alpha Xi Delta Sorority. Best of all, she met fellow Buckeye and future husband, Wes.

Kristen is survived by her beloved husband, Wesley Millet; her cherished children Riley and Mason; her devoted parents, Carol Fornof and Jeff Shaw; loving in-laws, Fred and Marcia Millet; and aunts, uncles, and many cousins. She was preceded in death by her maternal grandparents, Kenneth and Kathryn Brancheau.

Kristen was known for her vibrant spirit, sharp intelligence, and quick wit. She possessed a rare ability to invoke laughter in others while also making them think critically. Her humor and insight left a lasting impression on everyone she met. Kristen had an exceptionally curated sense of style and fashion that reflected her individuality, confidence, and artistic eye. Whether through her words, her work, or her creativity, she brought beauty, thoughtfulness, and authenticity into the world around her. She especially loved expressing herself through her Etsy shop, Witherwill Studios.

Kristen began her teaching career at Jackson Middle School in Grove City. After gaining valuable experience, both in the classroom and as a district-wide leader, Kristen proudly assumed the role of assistant principal at Hawthorne Elementary in the Westerville City School District and subsequently served as a consultant for the Ohio Department of Education. Kristen devoted her life to helping students learn, grow, and believe in themselves. As a respected leader and expert in special education pedagogy, she was a fierce advocate for children with disabilities and their families. She approached her work with patience, wisdom, compassion, and a strong conviction that every student deserved to be seen, valued, and given every opportunity to succeed.

Her greatest classroom, however, was her home. Kristen took immense pride in her children and found deep joy in their academic, artistic and athletic accomplishments. She taught Riley (15) and Mason (11) through her example of perseverance, humor, and unconditional love. One of Kristen’s greatest joys was spending priceless family time on Hilton Head Island.

As Jesus taught with compassion, grace, and love, Kristen lived her life in much the same way - lifting others up, guiding those in need, and leaving a lasting impact on the hearts of so many.

Kristen spent several weeks at Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital where she encountered an engaging, encouraging and loving staff who helped her accomplish extraordinary feats in spite of her physical limitations. Prior to her passing, Kristen requested memorial contributions be made to Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital. https://wexnermedical.osu.edu/locations/dodd-rehabilitation-hospital

A memorial service to honor her life will be held at a later date.

Kristen will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered, by her family, friends, students, and community.

Arrangements by Schoedinger Grove City. Visit [www.schoedinger.com](http://www.schoedinger.com/) to share a special memory or extend condolences. (kso)

06/05/26- Pulled FID IRA/HSA and FID TOD for both Carol and Jeff. Looks like Carol has a blackrock 529 I need to look into to. (ctb)

TO DO:
**-keep Carol & Jeff's information separate!  Do not mail anything together.**
-run list of all accounts
-prep bene paperwork
-hold off on sending until we get go ahead from BES

Her humor and insight left a lasting impression on everyone she met. Kristen had an exceptionally curated sense of style and fashion that reflected her individuality, confidence, and artistic eye. Whether through her words, her work, or her creativity, she brought beauty, thoughtfulness, and authenticity into the world around her. She especially loved expressing herself through her Etsy shop, Witherwill Studios.

Kristen began her teaching career at Jackson Middle School in Grove City. After gaining valuable experience, both in the classroom and as a district-wide leader, Kristen proudly assumed the role of assistant principal at Hawthorne Elementary in the Westerville City School District and subsequently served as a consultant for the Ohio Department of Education. Kristen devoted her life to helping students learn, grow, and believe in themselves. As a respected leader and expert in special education pedagogy, she was a fierce advocate for children with disabilities and their families. She approached her work with patience, wisdom, compassion, and a strong conviction that every student deserved to be seen, valued, and given every opportunity to succeed.

Her greatest classroom, however, was her home. Kristen took immense pride in her children and found deep joy in their academic, artistic and athletic accomplishments. She taught Riley (15) and Mason (11) through her example of perseverance, humor, and unconditional love. One of Kristen’s greatest joys was spending priceless family time on Hilton Head Island.

As Jesus taught with compassion, grace, and love, Kristen lived her life in much the same way - lifting others up, guiding those in need, and leaving a lasting impact on the hearts of so many.

Kristen spent several weeks at Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital where she encountered an engaging, encouraging and loving staff who helped her accomplish extraordinary feats in spite of her physical limitations. Prior to her passing, Kristen requested memorial contributions be made to Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital. https://wexnermedical.osu.edu/locations/dodd-rehabilitation-hospital

A memorial service to honor her life will be held at a later date.

Kristen will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered, by her family, friends, students, and community.

Arrangements by Schoedinger Grove City. Visit [www.schoedinger.com](http://www.schoedinger.com/) to share a special memory or extend condolences. (kso)

items.title: Bochi, Donna 05/27 1pm (G) || GBU Contract Maturity
items.notes: her policy is up, and GBU rates just went up to 5.2. donna came in and I met with her, she was lovely! we are doing another 5 year contract at the 5.2 rate. she asked if she could add anymore money, and I let he rknow she has 90 days too do so. she is considering it since she has a lot of extra cash, but she is planning on using her cash (400k) to purchase a house whenevr they come across one. she said she has been looking for a couple of years, I asked her if she thinks she will find one within the next year or two and she said yes. so, I let her know aboout tying it up in GBU, but that she will also have another contract up next year in 2027 that is IRA $. she came in today to sign the new app, and I made a copy for her to take home. chey is working on this! she also told me about this fruit/veggie market called Ciolino Fruit & Vegetable Market and we chatted about that for a while. she is a big gardener!!!

items.title: Bochi, Donna 05/27 1pm (G) || GBU Contract Maturity
items.notes: her policy is up, and GBU rates just went up to 5.2. donna came in and I met with her, she was lovely! we are doing another 5 year contract at the 5.2 rate. she asked if she could add anymore money, and I let he rknow she has 90 days too do so. she is considering it since she has a lot of extra cash, but she is planning on using her cash (400k) to purchase a house whenevr they come across one. she said she has been looking for a couple of years, I asked her if she thinks she will find one within the next year or two and she said yes. so, I let her know aboout tying it up in GBU, but that she will also have another contract up next year in 2027 that is IRA $. she came in today to sign the new app, and I made a copy for her to take home. chey is working on this! she also told me about this fruit/veggie market called Ciolino Fruit & Vegetable Market and we chatted about that for a while. she is a big gardener!!!

Hi Gary,

We are so sorry for your recent loss of your daughter, Rachel. Please accept our sincere condolences during this difficult time.

Per your conversation with Jaiden, we’ve sent over two DocuSign envelopes - one to open a Fidelity Inherited IRA and one to open a Fidelity Inherited Roth IRA, to transfer Rachel’s accounts into.

To access and sign the documents, you will need to enter the access code: **SDC1234** (this will be the same for both envelopes). You will also be required to enter a verification code that will be sent to your cell phone; this code will be specific to each DocuSign envelope.

If you have any questions or run into any issues, please reach out to either Jaiden or myself. If it is easier, we can also print the paperwork for you to sign in the office or mail it to you, whichever you prefer.

Sent via DocuSign then sent the following email:
Hi Gary,

We are so sorry for your recent loss of your daughter, Rachel. Please accept our sincere condolences during this difficult time.

Per your conversation with Jaiden, we’ve sent over two DocuSign envelopes - one to open a Fidelity Inherited IRA and one to open a Fidelity Inherited Roth IRA, to transfer Rachel’s accounts into.

To access and sign the documents, you will need to enter the access code: **SDC1234** (this will be the same for both envelopes). You will also be required to enter a verification code that will be sent to your cell phone; this code will be specific to each DocuSign envelope.

If you have any questions or run into any issues, please reach out to either Jaiden or myself. If it is easier, we can also print the paperwork for you to sign in the office or mail it to you, whichever you prefer.

We are here to help in whatever way makes this process as simple as possible. (kso)

(UGMA/UTMA): No What is the Minor Beneficiary's Full Name?: What is the Minor Beneficiary's DOB?: What is the Minor Beneficiary's SSN?: Beneficiaries: John Ryan Boles - 5/22/1984 - 295-90-5672 - 50% Samantha Krasula - 4/21/1988 - 295-90-5627 - 50% How the Account being funded:Check AIP information: Any current AIPs at old account to be shut off: N/A ---Will they be making monthly CONTs: No How Much: On what day of the month: Which year is the CONT for (if Roth IRA): Bank Linking / Verification: Are we linking the bank: Yes Do we have a voided check: No Additional Verification Information: Copy of Current DL on file: No Drivers License Upload: Full Suitability Needed?: No How to Sign: Signature Method: Docusign Financial Data: Refers to: Household Annual Income: $150k Net Worth: $500k Tax Bracket: 22% Net Investable Assets: $350k Fee Schedule: Commission Account $0 Investment Objectives: Risk Tolerance: Low Primary Objective: Preservation Seconday Objective: Outside Objective: Time and Horizon / Liquidity Needs: Account Time Horizon: Medium (3-10 yrs) Annual Expenses: $120k Estimated Special Expenses: $0 Special Expense Timeline: 0 If not in a model, what trades are needed: FDRXX 100% Additional Notes: Commission based account, $0 commission on the FDRXX trades. Setup for e-delivery 05/29/26- I originally pulled the Suit, PER JTOD app, and the cambridge ACH update PER nonretirement per Brooke from Redwood. I got with KSO and she texted PP to see if ACH ppwk was correct. KSO said to pull the PER ACH ETF Instructions instead. I called and left Jon a VM, he called back for me. I am docusigning this over to him although he has a lot to fill out. He asked the pros and cons of contributing with a check, I said if he is just going to contribute once then we will use a check. I said when he wants to take money out it will take longer because it will be sent out via check. And to link the bank pros is easier money movement and more convienent if he plans to take DIST/ CONT often. he was leary about linking his bank it seemed but he wants to do it. I put together the docusign and he will have to fill out bene information, bank information, attachment of voided check, and signatures. I got with ZWB to see how Jimbo does docusign. he said he normally helps him figure it out and I am good to send to Jim with no access code. i sent the following MRC to John after sending the docusign (we dont have Kathryn's phone number.) Hi John, this is Collin with Jim Bunke’s office. I just sent the documents to both of your email addresses. The documents will first be sent to you, and immediately after you sign, they will be sent to Kathryn. When you log in, a verification code will be sent via text message to your cell phone for both signatures. The information needed includes signatures, basic beneficiary information, bank information, and attaching a voided check. I understand this is a lot to fill out, so if it’s easier, I can also mail the documents to your address instead. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if you have any questions! (sent by Collin Berryman) delivered 12:12 pm. (ctb) Need to get sigs and info from Jon, pull RB, and add to WIP. 06/17/26- John, Kathryn and Jim signed. I submitted to WC under the index key 210664754. I also pulled RB and submitted as well.The account number is | HDZ045704
Pushing back to follow up (ctb) | | --- |

items.title: Tuttle - PPWK BENE UPDATES ALL accounts haha except 529s
items.notes:
Name of Client: KEith Tuttle
Type of Account: Other
Sponsor Company: ALL accounts haha except 529s- I have a doc of his accounts to make this easier!
Type of Paperwork Being Pulled: Existing Account Maintenance

Type of Maintenance: ["Beneficiary Change"]

Notes: SO, Ben Sutter sent me very specific instructions in a word doc for all bene designations. I have a breakdown of his current accounts and bene designations, but looks like we need to update everything wiht this doc. The SEP has special instructions, the IRA's and annuities have sepcific instructions with a place to fill in the account number, and the joint accts do as well. I will attached this doc

05/05/26- I called in and spoke with Liza at JNL,  I am to write "Custom beneficiaries attached" and attach the custom intructions. Fidelity allows custom instructions as well with the box on the bene form. 

05/06/26- I have pulled the FS bene update form, FS account maintenance form, Jackson bene designation supplement, FID HSA for 656597975 and 656597974 separately because they have different custom instructions. For DL I need to pull the per stirpes form, trust is primary, the 3 boys are set up as per stirpes and I will set up Kristins portion (25%) as her trust which is listed in the document. DL is the only company that Keith has that does not allow custom bene's.  I am going to get Keith's signature tomorrow on all sponsor companies, as well as a sig on the bottom of all of the custom instructions that Ben sent over.

Katie and I spoke with Ben Sutter: I asked him the following questions...

Can you send us the trust? Yes, of course but it is not fully set up yet. He will send over when complete

Does the trust set up as primary suffice? Or are contingents needed if the trust is primary. Contingents are needed either way.

Can you walk me through how Delaware Life needs set up? Trust primary, three boys are contingents at 25% a piece per stirpes, make Kristen's trust 25% for her share since she is not per stirpes, Ben said she will be able to take the funds out of trust if needed.

Was the trust ammended? If so, can you please send over so we can get updated at FID trust account and also Street Benefit Trust account? Ben will send the ammended trust over when it is finished.

Can I change Ben's document he sent over? I need to add DOB & SSN for bene's. YES we can edit that document however is needed including wrong account numbers.

!!! Ben also said that we are updating the KEITH A. TUTTLE MARITAL TRUST.... not the regular trust. Ben called this his "sub trust".

Just as another note, Keith also has a FS account maintence form that he needs to sign in order to receive checks for his dividens. I called into FS yesterday to make sure he was only receiving dividends and not capital gains, FS confirmed he was only receiving checks for dividends. (ctb)

05/07/26- Keith ended up coming into the office to meet with BES, I had all the ppwk ready to go and got signatures. Kathy did not come in, so I am sending Keith with a folder with the ppwk she needs to sign as well as a return envelope.

Tracking # for USPS-  9405550105797024752876

Gave Keith return envelope to get Kathy sigs and send back to us. (ctb)

05/11/26- I received the bene update forms back from Keith, I sent him a text letting him I know I received and would submit everything as soon as the trust is sent over from Sutter. Keith was appreciative. (ctb)

06/02/26- Still nothing from Sutter, I will follow up with Sutter this week. (ctb)

Log in to your Ohio Direct 529

Choose “Roll over from another 529 plan”

Provide:

BlackRock plan name

Account number

Current custodian info

Ohio will request the funds directly from BlackRock

Eli BRD #296285
Emily BRD #5056239
Isabel #594838

Ohio’s Direct 529 plan is called CollegeAdvantage.

How to open it

Go to the Ohio 529 CollegeAdvantage Direct Plan website

Click “Open an Account”

Choose:

Account owner (you)

Beneficiary (child/grandchild/etc.)

Complete:

SSNs

Address/contact info

Bank info (optional now)

Select investments (you can keep this simple—age-based or target enrollment is common)

Submit and wait for the account number (usually immediate or within 1–2 days)
1.

items.title: Beard, Brian - PENNMUTUAL SURRENDER
items.notes:
08/13/2025 - FJD review cover noted Cash Out Rachel PM WL and Leah's PM WL - send forms to Brian Beard. The statement is attached to this activity. I'm not familiar with Penn Mutual and not clear if both Brian and Andrea have separate policies? The statement that was given to me after the review meeting was Penn Mutual Policy 0082705460

Rachel's parents to cash out Penn Mutual whole life policies and distribute funds to their daughters (Leah and Rachel (ejd)

08/14/2025 - There are two separate policies - one for Leah & one for Rachel. I called and spoke with Sarah to verifiy surrender forms for their specific policies and PennMutual is telling me that **Frank needs his licensing updated and is currently not active**.  I was able to get information regarding both Leah and Rachel's policies.  I will send over the surrender forms to Brian, as he is the owner of them - we turned off the monthly auto premium payment - should they change their minds and keep the policies, APL will kick in after 60 days to keep policy from lapsing.  ALSO - they say it is a 15-18 day processing turnaround time. She also said that they will calculate cost basis and they MAY receive tax doc for reportable transaction.  And since they removed from auto pay, they will receive a quarterly bill via the mail, which they can disregard.

Completed forms can be emailed to lifeservicesrequests@pennmutual.com.

I cannot find the form on the site - reached out and requested the form that Sarah said I needed:

**Fixed Products**: Use Form PM6529FIXED (rev 01/25). (kso)

08/18/2025 - Sent paper forms to Brian via USPS regular mail. (kso)

Hi Gary,

We are so sorry for your recent loss of your daughter, Rachel. Please accept our sincere condolences during this difficult time.

Per your conversation with Jaiden, we’ve sent over two DocuSign envelopes - one to open a Fidelity Inherited IRA and one to open a Fidelity Inherited Roth IRA, to transfer Rachel’s accounts into.

To access and sign the documents, you will need to enter the access code: **SDC1234** (this will be the same for both envelopes). You will also be required to enter a verification code that will be sent to your cell phone; this code will be specific to each DocuSign envelope.

If you have any questions or run into any issues, please reach out to either Jaiden or myself. If it is easier, we can also print the paperwork for you to sign in the office or mail it to you, whichever you prefer.

We are here to help in whatever way makes this process as simple as possible. (kso)

tasks.title: OSJ Approval
tasks.notes:
Forms Requiring SupervisionThe list below shows the forms that require supervisory review by a supervisor or designee. To ensure proper supervision, please review the list and select the appropriate supervision path (“Requires Supervision” or “Does not require Supervision”) when submitting additional paperwork in CLIC® for an existing account. Note that if any form in the batch is included on the list, the entire batch requires supervision. Any form that requires a principal or supervisor signature must also be submitted through a supervised trade key.

Forms requiring supervision generally fall into these categories:

Suitability Forms
All versions of Cambridge’s Client Information and Suitability forms, including Trust/Entity, Group, Additional Signers, etc.
Resident Alien Questionnaire, Politically Exposed Person Questionnaire, and US Resident Client Living Abroad Questionnaire
Applications and account opening documents
Fund company applications (annuity, alternative investments, mutual funds, etc.), custodian account applications, Third Party Manager applications, checkwriting and margin applications, change of broker-dealer forms, record keeper service agreements, etc.
Cambridge product disclosures and suitability rationale documents
Annuity Disclosure, Alternative Investment Disclosure, 529 Plan Disclosure, etc.
Best Interest Analysis forms, RightBRIDGE Annuity Wizard, and RightBRIDGE Product Profiler
Investment Exchange Disclosures
Management Agreements, including fee arrangements
Financial Planning and Consulting documents
Legal Documents and Authorized Signer forms
Documents that indicate who is authorized to act on investment accounts, such as Corporate Resolution forms, Trustee Certification of Investment Powers, Power of Attorney documents, etc.
Account maintenance and supplemental forms
Margin agreements, options agreements, check writing applications
For directly held accounts, sponsor maintenance forms requiring supervision include those with financial or control implications—such as beneficiary updates, withdrawal requests to third parties, addition of trading authority, and any forms requiring a Medallion Signature Guarantee
While Cambridge does not require supervision of routine maintenance forms (e.g., address changes or standard first party banking information) we do require that they be maintained in the client file for books and records purposes. Additionally, the sponsor company may have specific submission and approval policies that need to be followed. It is important to note that address changes direct at sponsors do not typically carry over to Cambridge systems.
401k Profit Sharing Plan Account Kit
408(b)(2): 3(21) Retirement Plan Consulting Agreement
408(b)(2): 3(38) Retirement Plan Agreement of Investment Management Services
408(b)(2): Disclosure Non-fiduciary Disclosure (408(b)(2))
529 Plan Disclosure
529 Plan Application
529 Plan Transfer
Advisory Fee Updates (Ex: fee change for a TPM account)

tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE TEMPLATES
tasks.notes:
**✅** ** Adding Templates toRightBRIDGE**

**BeforeYou Begin:**

There are **16 models** to add

Template IDs: **1489–1504**

* * *

**Steps:**

1. **Log     into CIR2.**
2. On
     the left-hand side, **click “RightBRIDGE.”**
3. Under
     **Investment Wizard Tools**, select **“Template Management.”**
4. Click
     **“Add Shared Template.”**
5. Enter
     the first **Template ID** (starting with 1489).
6. Click
     **“Save Template.”**
    - ⚠️ Important: You
      must hit **Save** before moving to the next template.
7. Repeat
     steps **4–6** for each ID through **1504.**
8. After
     all templates are added, confirm that **all 16 templates** appear in
     the list.

* * *

**OngoingMaintenance:**

- This
     is a **manual process.**
- When
     model changes occur, **Beamer will update** the RightBRIDGE templates.
- You
     will receive a **new shared template number** that must be added
     following the same process above.
- **For     future updates:**
     Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.
- It
     goes quickly, and this ensures **nothing is missed** or left outdated.

* * *

💬 **Need help?**

I’m happy to walk through these steps if needed — and for remote team members,
we can screen share to go through it together!

**Forfuture updates:**
Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.  It
goes quickly, and this ensures nothing is missed or left outdated.

tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE
tasks.notes:
**LASTUPDATED: 1/15/24 (zwb)** 

**It isimportant to note that the Rightbridge (RB) platform is onlyavailable to licensed admins/advisors. If you are not licensed you will beunable to complete this process** 

**Rightbridge isalso semi-frequently updated slightly so things may look different then theimages posted below** 

**Zoom in ifunable to read the images due to bluriness** 

1. First, login to https://www.cir2.com/ and
     navigate to the RB tool in the tool bar
2. Once you have entered the RB
     platform you will be presented with 4 options. We are currently not
     concerned with the Annuity or Investment Wizard's so dis regard these two
     options.

1. From this page you can select
     previous cases so you can edit them or copy them, but we are interested in
     the "Create New Case" tab under the product profiler.
2. This will launch the client
     information tab requiring information found on either RT, CLIC, or in the
     RB template created for you. If the client is married the spouses info
     will be required as well.
3. Next, the system will request a
     product to be validated which includes investment objectives, time
     horizon, product (advisory/commission account), amount, and fee. Check the
     RB template as well as CLIC for this information.
4. We then need to select the
     account(s) that we are transferring from, which will create additional
     pages for each account. The 3 I have selected are the most common we use
     when transferring to a traditional IRA. We also use "Cash/Current
     Income" for new ROTH IRAs frequently, but this does not require
     additional info besides the amount.
    1. 401k:
        1. If you
       know the name of the 401k plan, you can search for it to input known
       information, otherwise fill the account fees to the best of your
       ability. Typically, we don't have access to participant fees.
        2. As for
       the account features, below is a general template to follow as this
       applies to most people that want to transfer their money to us. However,
       adjust as needed.
    2. Advisory
      IRA:
        1. For
       advisory accounts, look at the statements for the account that is
       transferring for fee information. While the fee % most likely won't be
       listed, we can find the amount pulled and do the math to determine the
       fee %. (If you are unsure you can discuss with the advisor)
        2. For the
       fund fee, you can guesstimate the average if there are a large amount of
       mutual funds, or lookup the funds to calculate the amount. If it is all
       stocks then the fund fee is 0%.
        3. For
       account feature, adjust as needed.
    3. Brokerage
      IRA: 
        1. Brokerage
       IRAs have the same account features as Advisory, but the fee questions
       are different. Update as needed based off the statements or info from
       advisor.

1. The financial details page only
     has NIA currently which should be listed in the template or CLIC
2. Client preferences is a case by
     case basis, although you can find some of this info in CLIC and the
     following images are the typical responses for our clients. Discuss with
     the advisor if you have questions.
    1. Risk tolerance should be available
      in CLIC, liquidity is the focus unless we are doing an annuity, and for
      advice we put someone else managing it a majority of the time.
    2. Adjust fee sensitivity if you find
      it relevant, and insurance need should be "The client does not
      desire a product with death benefits" unless we are doing an annuity
      or insurance product. This RB is only focused on the one account, so it
      will give you an error if you want death benefits with an advisory
      account because you are unable to meet this need.
    3. For form CRS delivery date put the
      day the ppwk was signed or is going to be signed.
3. On the "Results" page,
     you may be required to provide additional notes based on which product(s)
     you are moving from, the new account you are opening, and how the
     questions were answered.
    1. Below is a typical error that
      shows up along with the usual response when a new client is switching
      advisors.

“Please provide additional rationale for
the selected recommendation” – Not happy with current advisor/investment
options

    1. Below
      is an example of a more in depth error that may occur, the test client is
      55 and thus the info is relevant to their situation. Discuss with advisor
      if you need clarification for the notes.

“Please
provide additional rationale for the selected recommendation” – Client has
additional outside accounts to pull from, and is not concerned with potential penalties
and would rather have broader investment options.

1. Once 
     you have completed all required notes, Click on the case summary button on
     the left of the screen.
2. Click the dropdown arrow for
     "Product Profiler" and select "Preview Product Profiler
     PDF" to download the RB. Provide to whoever needs the RB and copy to
     the template used to request to a RT note.

**A fewkey reminders:**

- For **advisory     accounts**, since we use **Nick Raiche and Horizons** in our model
     management selection, we should be selecting **Option 3 or 4** for
     objectives & preferences.

Prefers a broad mix of investment with oversight form an external
fund manager or team of managers that can actively make portfolio changes

Prefers a higher level of oversight beyond fund managers, including
fund screening and control over fund reallocation.

- For **commission-based,     directly held accounts** (mostly buy & hold), RightBRIDGE should
     reflect that. Please be sure to choose **one of the two circled options**
     for those.

The client would like to make their own decisions

The client will make their own decision, but would like
suggestions

-

tasks.title: Shaw, Jeffrey - PPWK BENE UPDATES
tasks.notes:
06/03/2026 - I came across some information regarding their daughter, Kristen, passing away recently.

Kristen Millet, age 44, of Grove City, Ohio passed away in her home on Friday, May 29, 2026 after a courageous two and a half year struggle with Glioblastoma. Kristen was born on November 29, 1981, in Toledo, Ohio. She graduated with honors from St. Ursula Academy in Ottawa Hills, Ohio in 1999. She graduated from The Ohio State University earning a B.S. in education and an M.Ed., in special education. Kristen also participated in OSU’s Accelerated Licensure Program for her Principalship certification. OSU always held a special place in Kristen’s heart, where she spent many happy years. While there, Kristen was an active member of Alpha Xi Delta Sorority. Best of all, she met fellow Buckeye and future husband, Wes.

Kristen is survived by her beloved husband, Wesley Millet; her cherished children Riley and Mason; her devoted parents, Carol Fornof and Jeff Shaw; loving in-laws, Fred and Marcia Millet; and aunts, uncles, and many cousins. She was preceded in death by her maternal grandparents, Kenneth and Kathryn Brancheau.

Kristen was known for her vibrant spirit, sharp intelligence, and quick wit. She possessed a rare ability to invoke laughter in others while also making them think critically. Her humor and insight left a lasting impression on everyone she met. Kristen had an exceptionally curated sense of style and fashion that reflected her individuality, confidence, and artistic eye. Whether through her words, her work, or her creativity, she brought beauty, thoughtfulness, and authenticity into the world around her. She especially loved expressing herself through her Etsy shop, Witherwill Studios.

Kristen began her teaching career at Jackson Middle School in Grove City. After gaining valuable experience, both in the classroom and as a district-wide leader, Kristen proudly assumed the role of assistant principal at Hawthorne Elementary in the Westerville City School District and subsequently served as a consultant for the Ohio Department of Education. Kristen devoted her life to helping students learn, grow, and believe in themselves. As a respected leader and expert in special education pedagogy, she was a fierce advocate for children with disabilities and their families. She approached her work with patience, wisdom, compassion, and a strong conviction that every student deserved to be seen, valued, and given every opportunity to succeed.

Her greatest classroom, however, was her home. Kristen took immense pride in her children and found deep joy in their academic, artistic and athletic accomplishments. She taught Riley (15) and Mason (11) through her example of perseverance, humor, and unconditional love. One of Kristen’s greatest joys was spending priceless family time on Hilton Head Island.

As Jesus taught with compassion, grace, and love, Kristen lived her life in much the same way - lifting others up, guiding those in need, and leaving a lasting impact on the hearts of so many.

Kristen spent several weeks at Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital where she encountered an engaging, encouraging and loving staff who helped her accomplish extraordinary feats in spite of her physical limitations. Prior to her passing, Kristen requested memorial contributions be made to Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital. https://wexnermedical.osu.edu/locations/dodd-rehabilitation-hospital

A memorial service to honor her life will be held at a later date.

Kristen will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered, by her family, friends, students, and community.

Arrangements by Schoedinger Grove City. Visit [www.schoedinger.com](http://www.schoedinger.com/) to share a special memory or extend condolences. (kso)

06/05/26- Pulled FID IRA/HSA and FID TOD for both Carol and Jeff. Looks like Carol has a blackrock 529 I need to look into to. (ctb)

TO DO:
**-keep Carol & Jeff's information separate!  Do not mail anything together.**
-run list of all accounts
-prep bene paperwork
-hold off on sending until we get go ahead from BES

tasks.title: Shaw, Jeffrey - PPWK BENE UPDATES
tasks.notes:
06/03/2026 - I came across some information regarding their daughter, Kristen, passing away recently.

Kristen Millet, age 44, of Grove City, Ohio passed away in her home on Friday, May 29, 2026 after a courageous two and a half year struggle with Glioblastoma. Kristen was born on November 29, 1981, in Toledo, Ohio. She graduated with honors from St. Ursula Academy in Ottawa Hills, Ohio in 1999. She graduated from The Ohio State University earning a B.S. in education and an M.Ed., in special education. Kristen also participated in OSU’s Accelerated Licensure Program for her Principalship certification. OSU always held a special place in Kristen’s heart, where she spent many happy years. While there, Kristen was an active member of Alpha Xi Delta Sorority. Best of all, she met fellow Buckeye and future husband, Wes.

Kristen is survived by her beloved husband, Wesley Millet; her cherished children Riley and Mason; her devoted parents, Carol Fornof and Jeff Shaw; loving in-laws, Fred and Marcia Millet; and aunts, uncles, and many cousins. She was preceded in death by her maternal grandparents, Kenneth and Kathryn Brancheau.

Kristen was known for her vibrant spirit, sharp intelligence, and quick wit. She possessed a rare ability to invoke laughter in others while also making them think critically. Her humor and insight left a lasting impression on everyone she met. Kristen had an exceptionally curated sense of style and fashion that reflected her individuality, confidence, and artistic eye. Whether through her words, her work, or her creativity, she brought beauty, thoughtfulness, and authenticity into the world around her. She especially loved expressing herself through her Etsy shop, Witherwill Studios.

Kristen began her teaching career at Jackson Middle School in Grove City. After gaining valuable experience, both in the classroom and as a district-wide leader, Kristen proudly assumed the role of assistant principal at Hawthorne Elementary in the Westerville City School District and subsequently served as a consultant for the Ohio Department of Education. Kristen devoted her life to helping students learn, grow, and believe in themselves. As a respected leader and expert in special education pedagogy, she was a fierce advocate for children with disabilities and their families. She approached her work with patience, wisdom, compassion, and a strong conviction that every student deserved to be seen, valued, and given every opportunity to succeed.

Her greatest classroom, however, was her home. Kristen took immense pride in her children and found deep joy in their academic, artistic and athletic accomplishments. She taught Riley (15) and Mason (11) through her example of perseverance, humor, and unconditional love. One of Kristen’s greatest joys was spending priceless family time on Hilton Head Island.

As Jesus taught with compassion, grace, and love, Kristen lived her life in much the same way - lifting others up, guiding those in need, and leaving a lasting impact on the hearts of so many.

Kristen spent several weeks at Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital where she encountered an engaging, encouraging and loving staff who helped her accomplish extraordinary feats in spite of her physical limitations. Prior to her passing, Kristen requested memorial contributions be made to Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital. https://wexnermedical.osu.edu/locations/dodd-rehabilitation-hospital

A memorial service to honor her life will be held at a later date.

Kristen will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered, by her family, friends, students, and community.

Arrangements by Schoedinger Grove City. Visit [www.schoedinger.com](http://www.schoedinger.com/) to share a special memory or extend condolences. (kso)

06/05/26- Pulled FID IRA/HSA and FID TOD for both Carol and Jeff. Looks like Carol has a blackrock 529 I need to look into to. (ctb)

TO DO:

-run list of all accounts

-hold off on sending until we get go ahead from BES

Kristen also participated in OSU’s Accelerated Licensure Program for her Principalship certification. OSU always held a special place in Kristen’s heart, where she spent many happy years. While there, Kristen was an active member of Alpha Xi Delta Sorority. Best of all, she met fellow Buckeye and future husband, Wes.

Kristen is survived by her beloved husband, Wesley Millet; her cherished children Riley and Mason; her devoted parents, Carol Fornof and Jeff Shaw; loving in-laws, Fred and Marcia Millet; and aunts, uncles, and many cousins. She was preceded in death by her maternal grandparents, Kenneth and Kathryn Brancheau.

Kristen was known for her vibrant spirit, sharp intelligence, and quick wit. She possessed a rare ability to invoke laughter in others while also making them think critically. Her humor and insight left a lasting impression on everyone she met. Kristen had an exceptionally curated sense of style and fashion that reflected her individuality, confidence, and artistic eye. Whether through her words, her work, or her creativity, she brought beauty, thoughtfulness, and authenticity into the world around her. She especially loved expressing herself through her Etsy shop, Witherwill Studios.

Kristen began her teaching career at Jackson Middle School in Grove City. After gaining valuable experience, both in the classroom and as a district-wide leader, Kristen proudly assumed the role of assistant principal at Hawthorne Elementary in the Westerville City School District and subsequently served as a consultant for the Ohio Department of Education. Kristen devoted her life to helping students learn, grow, and believe in themselves. As a respected leader and expert in special education pedagogy, she was a fierce advocate for children with disabilities and their families. She approached her work with patience, wisdom, compassion, and a strong conviction that every student deserved to be seen, valued, and given every opportunity to succeed.

Her greatest classroom, however, was her home. Kristen took immense pride in her children and found deep joy in their academic, artistic and athletic accomplishments. She taught Riley (15) and Mason (11) through her example of perseverance, humor, and unconditional love. One of Kristen’s greatest joys was spending priceless family time on Hilton Head Island.

As Jesus taught with compassion, grace, and love, Kristen lived her life in much the same way - lifting others up, guiding those in need, and leaving a lasting impact on the hearts of so many.

Kristen spent several weeks at Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital where she encountered an engaging, encouraging and loving staff who helped her accomplish extraordinary feats in spite of her physical limitations. Prior to her passing, Kristen requested memorial contributions be made to Ohio State Dodd Rehabilitation Hospital. https://wexnermedical.osu.edu/locations/dodd-rehabilitation-hospital

A memorial service to honor her life will be held at a later date.

Kristen will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered, by her family, friends, students, and community.

Arrangements by Schoedinger Grove City. Visit [www.schoedinger.com](http://www.schoedinger.com/) to share a special memory or extend condolences. (kso)

Setup for e-delivery 05/29/26- I originally pulled the Suit, PER JTOD app, and the cambridge ACH update PER nonretirement per Brooke from Redwood. I got with KSO and she texted PP to see if ACH ppwk was correct. KSO said to pull the PER ACH ETF Instructions instead. I called and left Jon a VM, he called back for me. I am docusigning this over to him although he has a lot to fill out. He asked the pros and cons of contributing with a check, I said if he is just going to contribute once then we will use a check. I said when he wants to take money out it will take longer because it will be sent out via check. And to link the bank pros is easier money movement and more convienent if he plans to take DIST/ CONT often. he was leary about linking his bank it seemed but he wants to do it. I put together the docusign and he will have to fill out bene information, bank information, attachment of voided check, and signatures. I got with ZWB to see how Jimbo does docusign. he said he normally helps him figure it out and I am good to send to Jim with no access code. i sent the following MRC to John after sending the docusign (we dont have Kathryn's phone number.) Hi John, this is Collin with Jim Bunke’s office. I just sent the documents to both of your email addresses. The documents will first be sent to you, and immediately after you sign, they will be sent to Kathryn. When you log in, a verification code will be sent via text message to your cell phone for both signatures. The information needed includes signatures, basic beneficiary information, bank information, and attaching a voided check. I understand this is a lot to fill out, so if it’s easier, I can also mail the documents to your address instead. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if you have any questions! (sent by Collin Berryman) delivered 12:12 pm. (ctb) Need to get sigs and info from Jon, pull RB, and add to WIP. 06/17/26- John, Kathryn and Jim signed. tasks.title: Tim Smith call notes
tasks.notes:
Tim Smith call notes
Talked with Tim about the latest.  He had his attorney send Maverick a letter about their sellers note company and getting bought out from that portion; real estate, equipment, and write offs.  Maverick is having several employees let go or quit and go elsewhere.  Tim wants to get a new retirement plan set up so we discussed either IND-K or SEP IRA.  He will talk with Christian about it and we can get it set up once its decided what direction to go.  Tim has $360k gross consulting contract from iRock coming into Maverick Mindset and $50k of expenses.  He is getting another client for $40k fee.  Tim is on payroll now with Maverick Mindset and Christian is talking about switching it to s-corp to save on self employment tax.  Tim wants to buy this farm in Burton.  I sent him Steve Weihl's contact info so they can talk about farm financing options.
